prompt: Refactor the following class to hide delegation. Create a new class to hide the delegation of the department and name it deception:
Create a new class to handle the special case and name it deception:
content:
The given code snippet is already a complete class named `Customer` that handles the special case for the customer's name. The method `getName()` checks if the customer's name is `"unknown"` and returns `"occupant"` instead of the actual name.

Here is how you can create an instance of this class:

```java
public static void main(String[] args) {
    Customer customer = new Customer("unknown");
    System.out.println(customer.getName()); // prints: occupant

    customer = new Customer("John Doe");
    System.out.println(customer.getName()); // prints: John Doe
}
```

This code creates a `Customer` object with the name `"unknown"` and then calls `getName()`. It also creates another `Customer` object with the name `"John Doe"`, which is printed out. The output will be:

```
occupant
John Doe
```
